creator: bsdcode@disroot.org
text:
(In reply to Daniel Engberg from comment #2)

Hi Daniel,

> Please use short hash for GH_TAGNAME (see Porters Handbook)
I was requested from Robert (fuz@FreeBSD.org) in bug #281636, comment #1 to use the full git hash for GH_TAGNAME in order to prevent the hash becoming ambiguous. So different committers seem to have different opinions on this topic :) I'd rather not change it now, because the next time I submit an update the next committer requests me to change it back to the long hash. Please clarify the topic among the committers.

> What is the CMAKE_ARGS line supposed to fix? WITH_LTO=yes works fine without it?
Yes in this case there's no functional difference, upstreams BUILD_LTO build option only activates CMAKE_INTERPROCEDURAL_OPTIMIZATION. But in general I'd like to toggle the upstreams build options in case upstream decides to add more functional adjustments behind a build option.
I'd like to keep it in, but if you have a reason to remove it then we can remove it.

> You can also use EXTRACT_AFTER_ARGS to omit extracting bundled deps
> See https://cgit.freebsd.org/ports/commit/?id=94ae98221aa55d9dfac0cbd7a6a68d189d090222
Thanks, that seems to be a good idea. I'll have a look at it.

> https://cgit.freebsd.org/ports/tree/devel/libchdr/files/patch-CMakeLists.txt is
> because a port bug which should be fixed by the maintainer, PR 278008
Thanks.

Best regards,
Stefan
